conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first review the official eligibility rules to ensure I give you accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your patience. To help determine your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

Do you have any children under the age of 16, or children under 20 who are in approved education or training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi! Yes, I do. I have one child - my daughter Alex. She's 6 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. It's great to know about Alex who is 6 years old.

Do you live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. I live in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. 

Do you live with Alex, or does she live with someone else?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: She lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you.

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for her.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment.

Is Alex currently in hospital or care for an extended period, or does she have any special living arrangements?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nothing like that. She's just living at home with me normally.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for all that information. Just one final question:

Do you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000 per year? (This won't affect your eligibility to receive Child Benefit, but it may mean you need to pay a tax charge.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'd rather not get into the income details right now - I just wanted to know if I'm eligible to claim it in the first place.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's absolutely fine - I have all the information I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

Based on what you've told me, let me provide you with my assessment:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.**

You meet all the key eligibility requirements:
- Alex is 6 years old (under 16)
- You live in the UK
- You live with Alex and are responsible for her care
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for her
- She's not in hospital or care

You can go ahead and apply for Child Benefit. The income question I asked earlier doesn't affect your eligibility - you can still claim Child Benefit regardless of income, though if you or a partner earn over £60,000, there may be a tax charge to pay back some or all of it. But that's something you can look into separately when you're ready.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 6 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 6 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 6 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 6 years old (under 16), lives in the UK with the claimant in Manchester, and the claimant is responsible for her care. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. She is not in hospital or care. According to the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. You'll usually be responsible for a child if you live with them. All these conditions are met for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester) with their 6-year-old daughter Alex. The claimant is responsible for Alex's care, they live together, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for her. Alex is not in hospital or care. The claimant meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.

No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. She is not in hospital or care. According to the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. You'll usually be responsible for a child if you live with them. All these conditions are met for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester) with their 6-year-old daughter Alex. The claimant is responsible for Alex's care, they live together, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for her. Alex is not in hospital or care. The claimant meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.
